questions.question: What is biodiversity?
questions.correct_answer: The variety of plants, animals, and microorganisms found in a particular region
questions.explanation: Biodiversity includes all living organisms that interact with each other in ecosystems.

questions.question: Why are forests important for ecosystems?
questions.correct_answer: Forests support biodiversity and maintain ecological balance
questions.explanation: They provide habitat for wildlife and help maintain air, water, and soil quality.

questions.question: What is conservation?
questions.correct_answer: The protection and careful management of natural resources
questions.explanation: Conservation ensures sustainable use of forests and wildlife resources.
questions.source_section: Conservation of Forest and Wildlife in India

questions.question: When was the Wildlife Protection Act implemented in India?
questions.explanation: The act was introduced to protect wildlife and their habitats by banning hunting and trade.

questions.question: What was the main objective of Project Tiger?
questions.correct_answer: To protect and increase the declining population of tigers
questions.explanation: Project Tiger was launched to conserve tigers and their habitats.

questions.question: What are reserved forests?
questions.correct_answer: Forests that are highly protected and considered most valuable for conservation
questions.explanation: These forests are strictly regulated by the government.
questions.source_section: Types and Distribution of Forest and Wildlife Resources

questions.question: What are protected forests?
questions.correct_answer: Forests that are protected from further depletion
questions.explanation: These forests are safeguarded but may allow limited human activities.
questions.source_section: Types and Distribution of Forest and Wildlife Resources

questions.question: What are unclassed forests?
questions.correct_answer: Forests and wastelands belonging to government, private individuals, or communities
questions.explanation: These forests are not strictly categorized under reserved or protected forests.
questions.source_section: Types and Distribution of Forest and Wildlife Resources

questions.question: What was the Chipko Movement?
questions.correct_answer: A movement where villagers hugged trees to prevent them from being cut down
questions.explanation: It was a community-led effort to stop deforestation.

questions.question: What are sacred groves?
questions.correct_answer: Forest patches protected by local communities due to religious or cultural beliefs
questions.explanation: Sacred groves help conserve rare plants and animals.

notes.title: Biodiversity
notes.content: Biodiversity refers to the variety of life forms present in a particular habitat or ecosystem.

notes.title: Forest Conservation
notes.content: Conservation helps maintain ecological balance and preserve natural resources for future generations.
notes.source_section: Conservation of Forest and Wildlife in India

notes.title: Wildlife Protection Act
notes.content: The Wildlife Protection Act of 1972 was introduced to protect endangered species and regulate hunting.

notes.title: Forest Classification
notes.content: Forests in India are classified as reserved forests, protected forests, and unclassed forests.
notes.source_section: Types and Distribution of Forest and Wildlife Resources

notes.title: Community Conservation
notes.content: Local communities often play an important role in protecting forests and wildlife.

facts.fact: India is one of the world's richest countries in terms of biodiversity.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to conserve tigers.
More than half of India's forest land is classified as reserved forests.
facts.source_section: Types and Distribution of Forest and Wildlife Resources

facts.fact: Sacred groves are protected by communities due to cultural and religious beliefs.
